issues.description: Sandbox breakout in grep: _canonical_grep only _safe()-checked the root, then read every rglob descendant without confinement, so an in-sandbox symlink to an outside file would be read (and the relative_to/except ValueError branch relabelled the leak with its basename rather than rejecting it).
issues.verificationMethod: gpt-5.4 cross-provider code review of pull_verifier.py traced every filesystem dereference in each tool.
issues.suggestedTestOrCheck: grep over a sandbox containing a symlinked file/dir pointing outside must not surface the outside content.
issues.resolution_notes: Added _within_sandbox + _walk_files (os.walk followlinks=False) so symlinked dirs are never descended and every discovered file is real-path-confined before reading; a leaking file is skipped, not relabelled. Tests: TestGrepConfinement (3 cases).

issues.description: Deterministic-servant guard had an over-broad error exemption: when the canonical call also failed, the guard accepted ANY 'ERROR: ...' text, so a servant could inject model-authored text on a failing probe (missing file, bad regex) and pass.
issues.suggestedTestOrCheck: A servant returning a fabricated 'ERROR: <model text>' for a missing file must raise DeterministicServantViolation.
issues.resolution_notes: Introduced _canonical_result producing the FULL canonical ToolResult for success AND error; both servant and guard derive from it and require field-for-field equality (content/raw/elided/bytes_total). Test: test_fabricated_error_text_on_failing_probe_caught.

issues.description: _elide claimed a 60,000-byte cap but capped on character count (len(text), text[:cap]) and reported dropped chars, so multibyte UTF-8 content could exceed the advertised byte cap and weaken token-budget protection.
issues.verificationMethod: gpt-5.4 cross-provider review of _elide vs its docstring/contract.
issues.suggestedTestOrCheck: Elide multibyte content; assert the head's encoded byte length <= cap and bytes_total is in bytes.
issues.resolution_notes: _elide now encodes to UTF-8, caps on bytes, decodes a codepoint-aligned prefix (errors=ignore), and reports dropped bytes. Test: test_elision_caps_bytes_not_chars.

issues.description: When pull_route was called with config=None, _provider_config lazily loaded config but _pricing_for still received None, so configured model pricing was ignored and cost accounting silently fell back to the conservative table.
issues.verificationMethod: gpt-5.4 cross-provider review of pull_route config handling.
issues.suggestedTestOrCheck: pull_route with a config carrying model pricing must use that pricing, not the fallback.
issues.resolution_notes: pull_route now loads config once and passes the same resolved config to both _provider_config and _pricing_for. Test: test_pricing_uses_config_over_fallback. (The accompanying token/cost OVERSHOOT observation was accepted as inherent post-hoc behavior and documented as such in tool-contract.md section 5 rather than 'fixed'.)

issues.description: Tool-schema/parser inconsistency: _verdict_tool_schema marked summary required but _parse_verdict coerced a missing summary to '' and could emit a content-trivial entry the Set 066 artifact gate would reject.
issues.verificationMethod: gpt-5.4 cross-provider review of forced-verdict parsing.
issues.suggestedTestOrCheck: A verdict with empty summary AND no findings must be rejected; summary-or-findings must be accepted.
issues.resolution_notes: _parse_verdict now rejects a trivial verdict (empty summary AND no findings), guaranteeing every emitted entry satisfies the Set 066 per-entry content rule. Tests: test_trivial_verdict_rejected, test_verdict_with_findings_but_no_summary_ok.
